frameworks.name: recurrent neural network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: RNN state evolution analyzed via linearization around an operating point

frameworks.name: linearization of nonlinear dynamics
frameworks.one_line: two distinct linearizations (pre- vs post-nonlinearity) compared and related

frameworks.name: context-dependent neural computation
frameworks.one_line: gating and context effects visible in activity- but not activation-linearizations

core_question: How are the activation-based and activity-based linearizations of an RNN related to each other?
key_assumptions: RNN dynamics can be written in either activation or activity coordinates
pointwise nonlinearity is differentiable for local linearization
local linearization captures relevant dynamical structure near an operating point
manipulations: choice of coordinate (activations vs activities) for linearization
comparison of eigenstructures of the two linearized dynamics matrices
key_findings: the two linearizations yield different dynamics matrices
left and right eigenvectors of the two linearizations have a definable relationship
some context-dependent effects appear under activity linearization but not activation linearization
interpretations: coordinate choice matters when using linearization to interpret RNN computation
activity-based linearization can expose context-dependence hidden in activation-based linearization
limitations_noted: informal technical note rather than a full empirical study
